chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the Ranger code repository on GitHub (https://github.com/DependableSystemsLab/Ranger) under an MIT license in Section IV.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on software simulations using fault injection tools (TensorFI) against TensorFlow models running on standard GPU hardware. There is no mention of physical testbeds, real-world deployment with actual transient faults, or massive-scale cloud emulations beyond the described setup.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section II-C contains a dedicated 'Fault Model' subsection that formally defines assumptions: random single bit-flips in the data path (ALUs/pipeline registers), protected memory/cache via ECC/parity, and at most one fault per execution. It also explicitly addresses multi-bit flips in Section VI-B.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs. It quantifies performance overhead using FLOPs (Table IV) showing an average of 0.53%, analyzes memory overhead based on bound storage size, and includes a specific study (Section VI-A) analyzing the accuracy-resilience trade-off by varying restriction bounds.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is limited to software simulations and fault injection tools without any physical hardware deployment or real-world transient fault data.
